sanskrit: ब्रह्मण्याधाय कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा करोति यः। लिप्यते न स पापेन पद्मपत्रमिवाम्भसा॥
transliteration: brahmaṇy ādhāya karmāṇi saṅgaṃ tyaktvā karoti yaḥ | lipyate na sa pāpena padma-patram ivāmbhasā ||
literal_meaning: Having offered actions to Brahman, abandoning attachment, one who acts is not smeared by sin, like a lotus leaf by water.
contextual_meaning: When action is performed as an offering rather than as a claim of ego, the psyche remains uncontaminated by guilt or pride; the self is decoupled from karmic residue.
psychological_state: Flow-state: absorbed engagement without self-referential anxiety; actions feel 'clean' because outcome ownership is relinquished.
trigger_scenario: A nurse works a 12-hour ICU shift, calmly gives post-mortem care, then drives home without rumination because she mentally offered every procedure to the well-being of patients and to a higher purpose.
anchor_text: Work as an offering, untouched like a lotus leaf in water.
